name: Joe Sestak
subject: ANNOUNCEMENT: Walking in your shoes - across New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Sestak for President
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/75695/images/ADM_JOE_LOGO.png]

ANNOUNCEMENT - SUNDAY, OCTOBER 6TH

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/75695/images/sestak-walking-cropped.jpg][https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/75695/images/shoesign_blue%20without%20disclaimer.png]

***PLEASE FORWARD THIS TO EVERYONE YOU KNOW IN NEW HAMPSHIRE***

Since I announced my candidacy, I’ve talked about how we must unite this country through accountable leadership in Washington. And as Atticus explained to Scout in To Kill a Mockingbird , " You never really know a man until you stand in his shoes and walk around in them ." Not only have I believed this since I got into politics, but also in the U.S. Navy. It’s why, in 2015, I walked 422 miles across the entire breadth of my home state of Pennsylvania: to meet and understand people, and to show that I stood for them. And it’s why I’m announcing a walk across New Hampshire — 105 miles from Chesterfield, on the border with Vermont, to Portsmouth, where the harbor opens to the Atlantic Ocean, starting on October 13 th and ending October 20th.

After spending three months getting to know Iowans — driving 17,000 miles for 235 events and shaking nearly 30,000 hands — I want to listen to New Hampshirites and understand the challenges that they face as well, many of which are faced by working families across America. I’ll set out early every morning and finish late at night, walking along highways and byways, through towns and cities and rural communities. I'll be stopping along the way to talk with people about issues from education “training for a lifetime” to national security and healthcare (including the opioid crisis), and from small businesses and jobs to veterans and healthy habitats. I’ll be taking occasional breaks to stray from the route to attend other events throughout the day and evening — but when I do, I’ll put a stick in the ground and come back to the same point later on to continue the walk.

Join me — walk with me, attend any of the events below, or share my message on social media. We need a President who knows Americans, all Americans. We need a President who walks in their shoes and will unite America again.

[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/75695/images/Day0-Small.gif]

*****PLEASE FORWARD THIS TO EVERYONE YOU KNOW IN NEW HAMPSHIRE*****
Since I announced my candidacy, I’ve talked about how we must unite this country through accountable leadership in Washington. And as Atticus explained to Scout in _To Kill a Mockingbird_ , "**You never really know a man until you stand in his shoes and walk around in them**." Not only have I believed this since I got into politics, but also in the U.S. Navy. It’s why, in 2015, I walked 422 miles across the entire breadth of my home state of Pennsylvania: to meet and understand people, and to show that I stood for them. **And** **it’s why I’m announcing a walk across New Hampshire — 105 miles from Chesterfield, on the border with Vermont, to Portsmouth, where the harbor opens to the Atlantic Ocean, starting on October 13 th and ending October 20th.**
After spending three months getting to know Iowans — driving 17,000 miles for 235 events and shaking nearly 30,000 hands — I want to listen to New Hampshirites and understand the challenges that they face as well, many of which are faced by working families across America. I’ll set out early every morning and finish late at night, walking along highways and byways, through towns and cities and rural communities. I'll be stopping along the way to talk with people about issues from education “training for a lifetime” to national security and healthcare (including the opioid crisis), and from small businesses and jobs to veterans and healthy habitats. I’ll be taking occasional breaks to stray from the route to attend other events throughout the day and evening — but when I do, I’ll put a stick in the ground and come back to the same point later on to continue the walk.
**Join me — walk with me, attend any of the events below, or share my message on social media. We need a President who knows Americans, all Americans. We need a President who walks in their shoes and will unite America again.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/10611124/106923260/-84807227?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS83NTY5NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDM0MDViZS02NGU4LWU5MTEtYjVlOS0yODE4Nzg0ZDZkNjgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VHk2sddbi2adGXOHtng-RHKErMixKA8KB5K8SwlI-oE=&refcode=em100619map)
* **Exact times are subject to change and will be updated several days prior to events. **
* **We will be adding to this list as the days continue and also providing more details prior to each event.**

Thank you! = peterborough&amount=28 = montvernon&amount=46 = manchester&amount=62 = raymond&amount=77 = nhampton&amount=97 = portsmouth&amount=105 | ---|--- Respectfully, Joe ANNOUNCEMENT - SUNDAY, OCTOBER 6TH *PLEASE FORWARD THIS TO EVERYONE YOU KNOW IN NEW HAMPSHIRE* Since I announced my candidacy, I’ve talked about how we must unite this country through accountable leadership in Washington. And as Atticus explained to Scout in To Kill a Mockingbird , "You never really know a man until you stand in his shoes and walk around in them." Not only have I believed this since I got into politics, but also in the U.S. Navy. It’s why, in 2015, I walked 422 miles across the entire breadth of my home state of Pennsylvania: to meet and understand people, and to show that I stood for them. And it’s why I’m announcing a walk across New Hampshire — 105 miles from Chesterfield, on the border with Vermont, to Portsmouth, where the harbor opens to the Atlantic Ocean, starting on October 13 th and ending October 20th. After spending three months getting to know Iowans — driving 17,000 miles for 235 events and shaking nearly 30,000 hands — I want to listen to New Hampshirites and understand the challenges that they face as well, many of which are faced by working families across America. I’ll set out early every morning and finish late at night, walking along highways and byways, through towns and cities and rural communities. I'll be stopping along the way to talk with people about issues from education “training for a lifetime” to national security and healthcare (including the opioid crisis), and from small businesses and jobs to veterans and healthy habitats. I’ll be taking occasional breaks to stray from the route to attend other events throughout the day and evening — but when I do, I’ll put a stick in the ground and come back to the same point later on to continue the walk. Join me — walk with me, attend any of the events below, or share my message on social media. We need a President who knows Americans, all Americans. We need a President who walks in their shoes and will unite America again. Exact times are subject to change and will be updated several days prior to events. While Democrats are moving forward on an impeachment inquiry against a president who undermines our national security and tears at the fabric of our democracy, Donald Trump is raising millions of dollars off of it.
But that's not all, team. The Republican war chest is even larger once you account for the millions they have from conservative dark money groups that are coming together to defend him. Donald Trump is raising millions while Fox News pundits and conservative talking heads demonize Congress for upholding the constitution and proceeding with an impeachment inquiry.
**[Team, we deserve far better representation in Washington. If you believe that our country needs a president who answers to the people -- not those who line his pockets -- chip in whatever you can.](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-october?refcode=em_fr_20191006_CB_20191006-fr-CB-trumpmoney-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
I entered politics to be a voice of the people. I moved to Newark after law school because I wanted to advocate for the people and take on the slumlords who preyed upon vulnerable families living in the shadows of our society. I am here today to help those who don't get a seat at the table -- those who are too often forgotten once the dust settles on the campaign trail.
Here's my promise to you, friend: When I am in the White House, I'll be a president for the people -- **those who are ignored, overlooked, voiceless.**
From the beginning, I knew I wanted to run a campaign that didn't rely on dark money, federal lobbyists, corporate PACs, or fossil fuel or pharma execs -- and we've stuck by that. We rely on grassroots donations from across the country to fund our operation -- because that's the way it should be done. People-funded campaigns, built on the issues and values that unite us under common cause and voice. **But it 's no secret that we are being far outspent.**
**[So I'm asking you, friend, will you chip in $5 to help our campaign have the resources to fight back against conservative dark money?](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-october?refcode=em_fr_20191006_CB_20191006-fr-CB-trumpmoney-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**
Thank you for everything you do,
Cory
[](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-october?refcode=em_fr_20191006_CB_20191006-fr-CB-trumpmoney-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: Lindsey Graham is losing ground in SC -- FAST
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Peter, South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham, former Trump critic and current Trump sycophant, has been doing everything he can to cozy up to the White House \-- and it may cost him his re-election. Democratic challenger Jaime Harrison raised a whopping $2.6 million in the third quarter, breaking his own Q2 record for the most a Democratic South Carolina U.S. Senate candidate has raised. Graham called the entire whistleblower complaint "hearsay," labeling it a setup on "Face the Nation." He said that Trump's call with the Ukrainian president was "very appropriate" and that it was completely ethical for Trump to pressure a foreign country to dig up dirt for him. He's even defending Trump's trade war with China as his own constituents face layoffs and a slow economy as a result. His actions are having a tremendous impact on his re-election chances. Since June, Democratic challenger Jaime Harrison has cut the polling gap in half and is now only seven points down with 13 months left until Election Day. We're asking voters what they think about this race, and we need 139 more responses by MIDNIGHT: **Do you approve of Lindsey Graham?** [YES](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcddf2/6e9863e8/1551e412/1db0e653/1979049056/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/WpFPKpv-x") [NO](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcddf2/6e9863e8/1551e412/1db0e653/1979049056/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/WpFPKpv-x") Thanks for your support, WDTO Team [DONATE](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcddf2/6e9863e8/1551e412/1db0e651/1979049056/VEsF/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/when-democrats-turn-out-jaime-nd?refcode=WDTO20191006a.ND-b&refcode2=4EOdhCArkZ5XsmYB8LSD")

name: Team Hart
subject: Issue 3 of the Rita Review
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/RRXYh-lZGSbXTW9LFGd8BybyzGvSdghNfLxE9gaDbFs=/UdbmYN_ex0z2fUsdhp2AwOYT7KpkaXpwLIF1HfWXq7U=?refcode=191006-email&recurring=auto) _The spookiest thing about October is that we 're already in the fourth quarter of 2019. Buckle up, folks. This is the Rita Review._ **A Successful Third Quarter** September was the end of the third quarter, and thanks to you, we were able to meet our third-quarter grassroots fundraising goal! This sets us up for even more community events and campaign growth in the final leg of 2019. Thanks to everyone for your continued support -- it means the world to us. **Celebrating Labor** This Labor Day, we celebrated the hardworking men and women in Lucas, Clinton, the Quad Cities and Burlington. Working men and women built this country and I will always listen to the voices of those fighting for living wages, quality healthcare and dignified retirement. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/RRXYh-lZGSbXTW9LFGd8BybyzGvSdghNfLxE9gaDbFs=/UdbmYN_ex0z2fUsdhp2AwOYT7KpkaXpwLIF1HfWXq7U=?refcode=191006-email&recurring=auto) **More Late-Summer Festivals!** Rita and the team had the chance to attend Fort Madison's rodeo parade as well as Moulton's parade early in September. We also visited the Pulaski Corn Show where we had some great conversations and enjoyed the festivities. Then, we zipped up to the Davis County Iowa Democrats fall dinner, where Rita spoke to a fired-up group of local activists. When we say Rita is dedicated to listening to every voice in the district, we mean it -- that means going everywhere and to as many events as we can. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/RRXYh-lZGSbXTW9LFGd8BybyzGvSdghNfLxE9gaDbFs=/UdbmYN_ex0z2fUsdhp2AwOYT7KpkaXpwLIF1HfWXq7U=?refcode=191006-email&recurring=auto) **Pancake Days are the Best Days** Rita had a great time celebrating this awesome southern Iowa tradition of flipping pancakes and parading in Centerville while talking to the community and enjoying the perfect fall weather. Believe us when we say that Rita's talents only start with her community leadership, her ability to listen and her singing voice. She's a mean pancake flipper too! [](https://act.myngp.com/el/RRXYh-lZGSbXTW9LFGd8BybyzGvSdghNfLxE9gaDbFs=/UdbmYN_ex0z2fUsdhp2AwOYT7KpkaXpwLIF1HfWXq7U=?refcode=191006-email&recurring=auto) That's it for this month's Rita Review. Thanks for catching up with the campaign. We have a lot of great events coming your way this month, so keep a look-out for updates from the trail. Best,
Team Hart [CONTRIBUTE](https://act.myngp.com/el/RRXYh-lZGSbXTW9LFGd8BybyzGvSdghNfLxE9gaDbFs=/75-dvWa6CsnRtHuxwVNDoFpEu8RQ0KX6I0Lc1qD1FY4=?refcode=191006-email&recurring=auto) | Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa

name: Sara Gideon
subject: It's been exactly one year since Susan Collins cast her critical vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh.
body:
She still doesn't regret it.
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c7f/4bef7a87/6b495aa2/287f220a/2551722913/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Friends,
**Exactly one year ago today, Susan Collins cast a deciding vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court.**
Since then, it's become even more clear that the investigation into Brett Kavanaugh's past was a complete sham.
We now know that lawyers for one of the women who came forward about Kavanaugh during his confirmation hearing provided the FBI a list of 25 possible witnesses to his misconduct, but the FBI chose not to interview a single person.
And we know the Republican-controlled Senate imposed strict rules on the FBI, preventing it from expeditiously moving forward with its investigation. Despite the wildly incomplete investigation into his past - and despite Judge Kavanaugh's far-right rulings since joining the Court - Susan Collins still says she doesn't regret her vote to confirm him. She even said she was "sad" that her support for Kavanaugh cost her votes.
Well we're sad too. Sad that we have a Senator who puts Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump before Mainers._But we have a chance to change that._
[**Make a $3 contribution to my campaign for U.S. Senate today. Let 's show Susan Collins that we won't forget her vote, and we won't ever stop fighting. **](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c7f/4bef7a87/6b495aa2/287f2209/2551722913/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTEwMDYtYiJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-october?refcode=em191006-b")
Thank you for everything you've done so far to support our campaign. When we win this race and defeat Susan Collins next year, it will be because people like you supported us from the very beginning.
All my best,
_Sara_
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802c7f/4bef7a87/6b495aa2/287f2209/2551722913/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTEwMDYtYiJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-october?refcode=em191006-b")****

name: Sarah Lane, JeffMerkley.com
subject: Just a quick story about Jeff
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2cd69/6cd27893/524b5ff6/15874c31/2500875740/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | Hello my name is Sarah Lane and I work for Senator Merkley's campaign. Today is the one year anniversary of Brett Kavanaugh's confirmation to the Supreme Court. I'll never forget how I felt in the days leading up to the vote. Senators on both sides of the aisle knew that Christine Blasey Ford's allegations of sexual assault were credible, yet Republicans voted to confirm Kavanaugh anyway. This was an affront to sexual assault survivors and to women everywhere. Senate Republicans were sending a message that sexual assault doesn't matter. Everyone who has survived a sexual assault, or knows someone who has, understands that the trauma inflicted often lasts a lifetime. It was hard to stomach watching Republican Senators defend Kavanaugh, some of them even turning red in the face when coming to his defense, as if he were the victim in all of this. But not Jeff Merkley. He fought for us with everything he had. Jeff filed a court injunction to try and halt the confirmation, and he pressured other Senators to do the right thing. The night before the Senate voted to confirm Kavanaugh, Jeff even went to the Senate floor at 4 in the morning to read letters from sexual assault survivors. For hours, Jeff spoke to an empty Senate chamber reading letters he received from survivors. Jeff made sure that their stories would be included in the official congressional record. Jeff wanted their voices to be heard, even if other senators didn't think it was important. When I woke up the next morning and saw what Jeff had done in the wee hours of the night, it gave me a sliver of hope, that even in the darkest times in our country, we can find support in each other. I'm proud to be able to work for Jeff Merkley. He's one of those people who really does empathize with others. We could sure use more Jeff Merkleys in the Senate. _Sarah_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2cd69/6cd27893/524b5ff6/15874c2e/2500875740/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2019-10-06-kavanaugh&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2cd69/6cd27893/524b5ff6/15874c2f/2500875740/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2019-10-06-kavanaugh&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2cd69/6cd27893/524b5ff6/15874c2c/2500875740/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2019-10-06-kavanaugh&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2cd69/6cd27893/524b5ff6/15874c2d/2500875740/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2019-10-06-kavanaugh&refcode2=express-buttons")

name: Tina Smith
subject: [Need response from you] Do you support Trump?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Over the past two months, we've seen Trump and the GOP getting seriously organized in our state. But his actions are clear: Trump does not care about Minnesotans. So I have a question for you: Do you

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-oct2019?refcode=em191007survey2-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Team,

In just four days, Trump's hosting a rally right here in Minnesota.

In 2016, Trump narrowly lost our state by 1.5 points, and ever since, he has been determined to close that margin in 2020.

Over the past two months, we've seen Trump and the GOP getting organized in Minnesota. From money pouring in, to volunteer events (one with more than 250 people), and my opponent's tight relationship with the president -- he's even been called Minnesota's "mini-Trump."

Trump may want to win Minnesota, but his priorities are clear: He'll side with big corporations instead of Minnesotans time after time. And his attempts to undermine affordable health care will not slow down -- putting thousands of Minnesotans at risk of losing coverage.

Trump has his intentions, but his words and actions do not speak for Minnesotans. **So I have a question for you: Do you support Donald Trump?**
| | [YES »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/let-tina-know-do-you-support-donald-trump-?source=em191007survey2-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
---
| | [NO »](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/let-tina-know-do-you-support-donald-trump-?source=em191007survey2-full&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
---

Before Trump's rally, I want to know just how likely you think he will win here, if at all. It's easy to look at the news predictions and Trump's actions and assume the worst. But elections are decided by the people, and hearing from you helps to shed light on the reality.

Thanks for speaking up,

Tina

body:
Sign this petition in support of Democracy Dollars if you believe that elections shouldn’t be bought out by the highest bidder or corporations with special interests.

| [](http://act.yang2020.com/go/68?t=1&akid=1363%2E377263%2Ee6Fufv) **Elections should be funded by the American people, not the 1% and corporations.** [ADD YOUR NAME TO SUPPORT DEMOCRACY DOLLARS](https://act.yang2020.com/sign/email-democracydollars-petition-20191006?source=email-20191006am-all&t=18&akid=1363%2E377263%2Ee6Fufv) Yang Gang --

If you follow American politics, you know that in almost every election cycle, a candidate comes along promising to end corruption by fixing how we finance our elections.

The message of campaign finance reform resonates because in our hearts we know the system is rigged. Citizens United opened up the floodgates to mega-donors and corporations funding House & Senate candidates that grassroots donors could never compete with.

For these reasons, I’ve proposed a **Democracy Dollars** initiative. The citizens of this country need to become our most powerful lobbyists. The voice of the people is now drowned out by corporate money and special interests. That is what we must reverse.

A Democracy Dollars proposal that puts $100 into every voter’s pocket each year to contribute to campaigns and candidates would be an extremely effective way to overcome corporate lobbyists and mega-donors.** It would washout lobbyist cash by a factor of 8-to-1 and restore the power of our democracy into the people’s hands.**

[Sign this petition in support of Democracy Dollars if you believe that elections shouldn’t be bought out by the highest bidder or corporations with special interests.](https://act.yang2020.com/sign/email-democracydollars-petition-20191006?source=email-20191006am-all&t=20&akid=1363%2E377263%2Ee6Fufv) | [SIGN THE PETITION](https://act.yang2020.com/sign/email-democracydollars-petition-20191006?source=email-20191006am-all&t=19&akid=1363%2E377263%2Ee6Fufv)
---
This campaign is about making our government accountable to the people it is elected to serve, not the super-wealthy or the lobbyists it currently works for. This isn’t radical, it’s common-sense.

Thanks for fighting with me,

-Andrew
**Most of our donations come from people like you chipping in small amounts when they can. Your continued generosity helps us keep building this movement.** | [Contribute](https://act.yang2020.com/go/12780?t=2&akid=1363%2E377263%2Ee6Fufv)

subject: #MoscowMitch has hobbled the FEC -- and opened up our elections to a free-for-all
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Hello Peter, The number of Federal Election Commission (FEC) commissioners dropped to 3 at the end of August, bringing the nation's election regulatory body below the stipulated quorum of 4. They now cannot hold meetings, issue fines, or clarify rules that keep our elections as fair as possible \-- opening the door for campaigns to ignore finance regulations without any repercussions. Foreign governments will have an easier time interfering in the 2020 presidential elections and GOP dark money will flow freely. This is no accident \-- it's part of a carefully orchestrated assault on democracy by national Republicans, specifically #MoscowMitch McConnell. He's the one who has stood in the way of campaign finance reform, spearheaded the Citizens United case, and blocked any new appointments to the FEC. He is actively tearing down every protection that props up our democracy and even standing in the way of election protections that would stop Russia from meddling in 2020. If you believe that our country needs a fully functioning FEC and Democrats need to fight back against corrupt politicians like Mitch McConnell, we need your donation NOW. | Friend,

One year ago today, Cory Gardner cast a decisive vote to confirm Brett Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court.

Let's never forget that, team.

Despite numerous credible accusations of sexual assault against him, and after one of the most moving hearings we've ever seen, Gardner voted to give Kavanaugh a lifetime appointment to the highest court in the land.

That's especially poignant in light of news this week that the Supreme Court is going to hear a major case on reproductive rights sometime next year.

Here's the bottom line: _I never would have voted to confirm Brett Kavanaugh._

**Next year we have the opportunity to take back the Senate and stop Mitch McConnell from appointing any more conservative judges once and for all.
